title: betula pubescens var. odorata
intro: Betula pubescens var. odorata, also known as sweet birch, is a variety of the downy birch tree, characterized by its sweet, wintergreen-scented resin. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, containing salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides medicinal properties and relief from pain and inflammation. The health benefits of sweet birch include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating conditions such as arthritis, fever, and skin irritations. Sweet birch is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and infusions, and is also used in traditional perfumery and flavoring due to its distinctive wintergreen scent.
